name: Fezzan Solar Hub
description: Southern desert region with exceptional solar irradiance. GHI >7 kWh/m²/day year-round. Flat terrain ideal for large-scale PV deployment.
rationale: Highest GHI in Libya (~2,600+ kWh/m²/year), flat terrain, low population density, proximity to Sabha grid
challenges: Remote location, sand/dust management, distance from coastal demand centers, limited road infrastructure

name: Jufra Solar Park
description: Central Libyan plateau with outstanding solar resource. Strategic mid-country location between northern demand and southern generation.
rationale: GHI ~2,500 kWh/m²/year, flat desert terrain, strategic location on north-south transmission corridor
challenges: Distance from grid connection, extreme summer temperatures, water scarcity for panel cleaning

name: Misrata Peri-Urban Solar
description: Distributed solar near Libya's third-largest city. Direct gas displacement at the adjacent 650 MW Misrata OCGT plant.
rationale: Adjacent to existing 650 MW gas plant, established grid connection, high local demand, GHI ~2,100 kWh/m²/year
challenges: Land use competition near urban area, higher land costs, requires coordination with GECOL dispatch

name: South Tripoli Solar Belt
description: Solar development zone south of the capital near existing 500 MW gas plant. Maximum gas displacement impact for Libya's largest demand center.
rationale: Highest demand center in Libya, adjacent to South Tripoli 500 MW plant, strong grid infrastructure, GHI ~2,050 kWh/m²/year
challenges: Urban sprawl encroachment, dust from construction, security considerations, land acquisition

name: Benghazi Outskirts Solar
description: Utility-scale solar south of Benghazi serving eastern Libya's largest city. Paired with North Benghazi 740 MW gas plant.
rationale: Eastern Libya's main demand center, existing 740 MW gas plant for displacement, GHI ~2,150 kWh/m²/year, flat terrain south of city
challenges: Security situation, coordination with eastern grid operator, dust management

name: Darnah Coastal Wind Farm
description: High-potential coastal wind site at Darnah (Derna). Mediterranean coastal winds with measured mean speeds of 7-9 m/s at hub height.
rationale: Strongest measured coastal winds in Libya (7-9 m/s at 80m), proximity to Derna Power Station, favorable terrain channeling
challenges: Coastal corrosion environment, seismic zone, terrain complexity for large turbine transport, heritage site proximity

name: Al Bayda Highland Wind
description: Elevated plateau wind site in the Jebel Akhdar (Green Mountain) region. Orographic wind acceleration creates strong, consistent resource.
rationale: Elevated terrain (600m+) creates orographic wind acceleration, 6.5-8 m/s mean wind speed, existing road infrastructure
challenges: Complex terrain for turbine installation, potential visual impact on Green Mountain landscape, access road requirements

name: Tarhunah Wind Corridor
description: Inland wind corridor southeast of Tripoli. Topographic channeling between the Nafusa Mountains and coastal plain creates reliable wind resource.
rationale: Topographic wind channeling, 6-7.5 m/s at hub height, proximity to Tripoli demand center, flat ridgeline terrain
challenges: Agricultural land use conflict, turbine transport logistics to elevated site, intermittent dust storms

name: Gulf of Sirte Hybrid Park
description: Combined solar + wind facility on the Gulf of Sirte coast. Complementary generation profiles: solar peaks midday, onshore winds peak evening/night.
rationale: Complementary solar-wind profiles improve capacity factor, existing Sirte grid infrastructure, coastal wind + desert solar zone overlap
challenges: Conflict-affected area requiring security assessment, salt spray on solar panels, dual-technology maintenance complexity

name: Zawiya Industrial Hybrid
description: Solar-wind hybrid near Zawiya refinery and industrial zone. Provides direct industrial power and displaces gas at the adjacent 660 MW plant.
rationale: Adjacent to 660 MW Zawiya gas plant and oil refinery, high industrial load, coastal wind + solar complementarity
challenges: Industrial zone land constraints, refinery proximity safety setbacks, air quality impacts on solar yield

name: Ghadames CSP Tower
description: Concentrated Solar Power with thermal storage in the western Sahara near Ghadames. DNI exceeds 2,700 kWh/m²/year. Molten salt storage enables dispatchable generation.
rationale: Exceptional DNI >2,700 kWh/m²/year, flat terrain, thermal storage enables dispatchable baseload, potential Algeria-Libya interconnection
nearestGrid: Ghadames local grid (requires new 220kV line to Nalut, ~200 km)
challenges: Extreme remoteness, water requirements for steam cycle, high capital cost, new transmission required, sand abrasion on mirrors

name: Kufra Deep Desert Solar
description: Ultra-high irradiance site near the Kufra oasis in southeastern Libya. Among the highest solar resources globally. Powers local agricultural and water pumping loads.
rationale: GHI >2,700 kWh/m²/year, existing settlement and agricultural demand at Kufra oasis, displaces expensive diesel generation
nearestGrid: Kufra local grid (isolated from national grid)
challenges: Completely isolated from national grid, extreme remoteness, logistical supply chain, limited local technical capacity
